body:
## Goal
Provide a dependency-free Node.js JSON REST API where users sign up, sign in for an opaque bearer session, and manage their own personal notes, with all data surviving a server restart.

## Spec
ESM, Node built-ins only — no external web framework. Server listens on process.env.PORT (default 3000). Persistence is an on-disk store (embedded SQLite file via node:sqlite, or an equivalent atomically-written on-disk store) — NOT in-memory; data survives restart. All request/response bodies are JSON.

Endpoints:
- POST /signup {username, password} -> 201 {id, username}. Reject duplicate username and missing/blank fields with 4xx.
- POST /login {username, password} -> 200 {session} where session is an opaque credential (unguessable token). Wrong credentials -> 401.
- GET /me -> 200 {id, username} for the caller identified by Authorization: Bearer <session>. Missing/invalid session -> 401.
- POST /notes {title, body} -> 201 with the created note (including its id and owner association). Requires a valid session.
- GET /notes -> 200 JSON array of ONLY the caller's own notes. Requires a valid session.

Invariants: passwords are never stored in plaintext (hash+salt); a note is only ever visible to its owner (no cross-user leakage); malformed JSON, missing fields, and unknown routes return sensible 4xx status codes rather than crashing the process. An npm "start" script launches the server.

## Changes
- `src/server.js` — creates
- `src/store.js` — creates
- `test/api.test.js` — creates
- `package.json` — refactors-existing

## Acceptance
- [ ] AC-1: POST /signup with {username,password} creates a user and responds 201 with JSON {id, username}; a second signup with the same username is rejected with a 4xx status.
- [ ] AC-2: POST /login with correct credentials responds 200 with JSON {session} carrying an opaque token; login with wrong credentials responds 401.
- [ ] AC-3: GET /me with a valid Authorization: Bearer <session> responds 200 with the caller's {id, username}; a missing or invalid session responds 401.
- [ ] AC-4: POST /notes with {title,body} and a valid session responds 201 with the created note; GET /notes responds 200 with a JSON array containing only that caller's own notes and never another user's notes.
- [ ] AC-5: Malformed JSON bodies, missing required fields, and unknown routes return sensible 4xx status codes without crashing the server.
- [ ] AC-6: Users and notes created before a server restart are still present and usable after restarting the process (on-disk persistence, not in-memory).
- [ ] AC-7: The server binds to process.env.PORT when set and defaults to 3000, and `npm start` launches it.

## Verify
- npm test (e2e)
- npm run typecheck (validate)
- npm run lint (validate)

## Non-Goals
- No web/HTML UI — JSON API only.
- No session expiry, refresh tokens, or logout endpoint.
- No note update/delete endpoints (only create and list).
- No external dependencies or web framework.
- No rate limiting, HTTPS/TLS termination, or CORS handling.

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"A multi-user notes service needs authenticated accounts and per-user note isolation backed by durable storage, delivered without external framework dependencies."} -->

<!-- plan-story: b1b25f3160d1ae82 -->

title: JSON REST API for user accounts and personal notes with persistent on-disk store
